title: Mid session 2901 T224 C
body:
NAME OF CANDIDATE: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . STUDENT NUMBER: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .

THE UNIVERSITY OF NEW SOUTH WALES

SCHOOL OF MATHEMATICS AND STATISTICS

Term 2, 2024

MATH2901 Higher Theory of Statistics Mid-session test C

(1) TIME ALLOWED – 50 minutes

(2) TOTAL NUMBER OF QUESTIONS – 3

(3) ANSWER ALL QUESTIONS

(4) THE QUESTIONS ARE OF EQUAL VALUE

(5) THIS PAPER MAY NOT BE RETAINED BY THE CANDIDATE

(6) Write your answers on the test paper in space provided. You may use your own calculators. You are allowed a one-sided (A4) handwritten formula sheet of your own. Ask if you need more paper.

All answers must be written in ink. Except where they are expressly required pencils may only be used for drawing, sketching or graphical work.  Term 2, 2024 MATH2901 Page 2

1. [10 marks] Let N ∼ Bin(10; 1/2) be the number of trips a courier needs to make into the business district each day. The courier observed that Xi , the number of stops he has to make in front of traffic lights in his ith trip of the day, is independent of the previous trips, and follows the Poisson distribution with intensity parameter 2i − 1 (the intensity increases with i, as the traffic gets heavier as the day progresses).

a) [5 marks] Today the courier needed to make n trips to the business district. Find the expected number of stops he made in front of traffic lights. (Hint: ni=1 i = n(n+1) P )

b) [5 marks] Find the expected number of stops he has to make in front of traffic lights on a random day. (Hint: use the Law of Iterated Expectations)

Term 2, 2024 MATH2901 Page 3

2. [10 marks] Let (X1 , X2 ) be a continuous bivariate vector with joint density

fX1 X2 (x1 , x2 ) = ce−x2 1I{0≤x1 ≤x2 <∞}

for a constant c > 0.

a) [3 marks] Determine the normalisation constant c.

b) [2 marks] Are X1 and X2 independent? Justify.

Term 2, 2024 MATH2901 Page 4

c) [2 marks] Find the marginal density of X2 . Deduce the distribution of X2 and its expectation E(X2 ).

d) [3 marks] Find the conditional density fX1 |X2 (x1 |x2 ). Deduce the distribution of X1 given X2 = x2 , and the conditional expectation E(X1 |X2 = x2 ).

Term 2, 2024 MATH2901 Page 5

3. [10 marks] A sieve with diameter d is used to separate a large number of blueberries into two classes: ‘small’ (diameter smaller than d) and ‘large’ (diameter greater than d). Suppose that the diameters of the blueberries are normally distributed with expectation E(D) = 1 cm and variance Var(D) = 0.01 cm2 .

a) [4 marks] Find the diameter d of the sieve such that the proportion of blueberries classified as ‘large’ is 30%. (Hint: Φ(−1.0364) = 0.15, Φ(−0.5244) = 0.30, Φ(0.5244) = 0.70, Φ(1.0364) = 0.85)

Term 2, 2024 MATH2901 Page 6

b) [6 marks] Suppose that the diameter is chosen as in a). Use the Central Limit Theorem to obtain an approximation of the probability that out of n = 1000 blueberries, at most 280 will be classified as ‘large’. (Hint: Φ(0.28) = 0.6103, Φ(0.30) = 0.6179, Φ(0.5244) = 0.70, Φ(1.3448) = 0.9106, Φ(1.3456) = 0.9108, Φ(1.3801) = 0.9162)
